Description: The document must include both a quickstart guide and a full API reference section.
Notes: AND — both sections must be present. Evaluate as composite.

Description: Installation instructions must be provided with platform-specific commands.
A getting-started code example must be present AND must be runnable without modification.
Notes: AND — presence alone is not sufficient; the example must be self-contained.

Description: The document must include either a table of error codes OR inline error message descriptions for each endpoint.
Notes: OR — either format is acceptable. Check for at least one.

Rule Name: Authentication via Header OR Query Param
Description: Authentication documentation must describe header-based auth OR query-parameter auth.
Notes: OR — document at least one method.

Rule Name: SDK Examples in Python AND JavaScript
Description: Code examples must be provided in both Python AND JavaScript. Other languages are optional.
Notes: AND — both languages required. Missing either is auto-fail.

Description: The document must state the versioning policy (semver, calver, or custom).
Rate limits must be documented with specific numbers (requests per minute or per hour).
If the API returns lists, pagination must be documented with cursor OR offset parameters.
If webhooks are supported: event types must be listed AND retry/backoff policy must be described.
Notes: AND — both required if webhooks exist. Skip if no webhooks.

Description: The documentation must include a search feature or index.
Every endpoint must have its response schema documented with field names AND types.
Notes: AND — field names alone are insufficient.
